summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a 'scatter plot'?

A scatter plot displays data points as individual dots on a coordinate plane, allowing you to visually identify patterns like linear, quadratic, or exponential trends.

What is a 'mathematical model'?

A mathematical model is an equation or function that approximates real-world data, allowing us to analyze trends and make predictions.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A student examines three data sets and calculates: Data Set 1 has constant first differences of 4, Data Set 2 has constant ratios of 1.5, and Data Set 3 has constant second differences of 6. Which models should be used?

Constant first differences indicate linear, constant ratios indicate exponential, and constant second differences indicate quadratic -- these are the three key tests for choosing a model.
